run_a.wiki_after_a:
# Acme — Master Services Agreement

## Parties & Roles
- **Our client (BUYER / Customer)**: Northwind Trading Ltd
- **Counterparty (Vendor / Supplier)**: Acme Corp
- **Counterparty's counsel**: Smith Crowell LLP

## Document in Play
- **Acme-MSA.txt** — Master Services Agreement (3 pages; appears to be a short-form or extract version)

## Headline Commercial Terms

| Term | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Initial Term** | 24 months from Effective Date |
| **Renewal** | Automatic 12-month rollovers; 90 days' written notice to opt out |
| **Payment** | 30 days from invoice; late interest at 4% p.a. above Bank of England base rate |
| **Liability Cap** | Aggregate liability capped at fees paid in preceding 12 months; exceptions for breach of confidentiality and indemnification obligations. No indirect / consequential damages exclusion applies. |
| **Governing Law / Jurisdiction** | England and Wales; exclusive jurisdiction of English courts |

## Notable Observations
- The agreement text is brief — sections identified so far cover Term (s.1), Fees & Payment (s.2), Limitation of Liability (s.7), and Governing Law (s.12). Key areas not yet seen include: scope of services, warranties, IP ownership, indemnification (referenced but not detailed), data protection, termination for cause, and confidentiality — these may be in attachments, SOWs, or a fuller version of the document.
- Governing law is England & Wales, consistent with an English-law transaction.

pinned_bodies: Correction: we are NOT taking the auto-renewal as-is — the lawyer requires a right to terminate for convenience on 60 days' notice. Treat this as settled.
